title: COP26: Amid Climate Alarm, Belfer Experts Are Helping the World Decarbonize
summary: World leaders are gathering in Glasgow next week to build momentum around three climate change goals. At the Belfer Center, our economists, scientists, energy experts, intelligence analysts, and environmental practitioners have been helping policymakers and the public better understand climate change for years. We’ve also been advancing timely research and ideas to help humanity both cope with this challenge and tackle it head on.
body: World leaders are gathering in Glasgow from Oct. 31 through Nov. 12 to build momentum around three climate change goals: 1) Secure global net zero by 2050 and limit global temperature increases to 1.5 degrees C.; 2) Adapt to protect communities and natural habitats; 3) Mobilize at least $100 billion in climate finance per year. These are ambitious goals, with difficult implementation plans and complex tradeoffs. Yet scientists and stakeholders agree that the cost of inaction is unacceptably high, calling the 2020s the “decisive decade” to halt an “existential” challenge. At the Belfer Center, our economists, scientists, energy experts, intelligence analysts, and environmental practitioners have been helping policymakers and the public better understand climate change for years. We’ve also been advancing timely research and ideas to help humanity cope with this challenge and tackle it head on. Climate change is a multi-faceted crisis, demanding insights from a range of disciplines. In that spirit, we offer here highlights of recent and current work dedicated to solving the biggest collective action problem in human history. The latest from Belfer See more Insights See the full quote in Foreign Affairs See the full quote in An Economic View of the Environment See the full quote in Is China's Hydrogen Economy Coming? Hear the full quote in Environmental Insights Events Belfer Center experts are holding events about COP26, both in Glasgow and here in Cambridge. Blindspots of Net-zero Strategies and How to Address Them Monday, November 1, 2021 | 8:30AM GMT Prospects for Article 6: COP-26 and Beyond Monday, November 1, 2021 | 9:00am - 10:15AM EDT Cultivating Climate: US-China Relations and Global Climate Cooperation Monday, November 1, 2021 | 12:15-2:00PM EDT Securing Climate Ambition with Cooperative Approaches: Options Under Article 6 Wednesday, November 10, 2021 | 4:45 – 6:00PM GMT Publications Advancing International Cooperation under the Paris Agreement: Issues and Options for Article 6 Michael A. Stavins, Doug Gavel Harvard Project on Climate Agreements Co-hosts COP26 Side Event on Securing Climate Ambition through Cooperative Approaches Facilitated by Article 6 After World Leaders Depart, Hard Talks Begin at COP26 COP26: Amid Climate Alarm, Belfer Experts Are Helping the World Decarbonize Print

_list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: The article discusses the ambitious climate goals set for COP26 in Glasgow, aiming to secure global net zero by 2050, limit temperature rise to 1.5°C, adapt to protect communities and habitats, and mobilize significant climate finance. It emphasizes the 'decisive decade' of the 2020s to address this existential challenge, highlighting the high cost of inaction. The Belfer Center's multidisciplinary experts—including economists, scientists, and energy analysts—are contributing research and insights to help policymakers understand and tackle climate change. Their work involves advancing international cooperation under mechanisms like Article 6 of the Paris Agreement, analyzing carbon pricing and just transitions, and exploring low-carbon energy systems, such as in China. The center is also hosting related events during COP26 to foster dialogue on implementation strategies and global security implications.
result.main_content_zh: 本文围绕在格拉斯哥举行的COP26气候大会，阐述了其三大核心目标：到2050年实现全球净零排放、将温升控制在1.5°C以内、适应气候变化以保护社区和自然栖息地，以及每年动员至少1000亿美元的气候资金。文章指出，2020年代是应对这一生存挑战的“决定性十年”，不作为的代价极高。贝尔弗中心的跨学科专家团队，包括经济学家、科学家和能源专家，正通过研究和分析帮助政策制定者理解和应对气候变化。他们的工作涉及推动《巴黎协定》第六条下的国际合作，分析碳定价与公正转型，并探索低碳能源系统（如中国的氢经济）。中心还在COP26期间举办相关活动，就实施策略、全球安全影响等议题促进对话。
